# SaaS Terms of Service: Section Checklist and Best Practices (2026)

## Summary

Practitioner guide covering the essential structure of SaaS Terms and Conditions. Confirms the 10-section canonical structure expected in modern SaaS agreements and common mistakes founders make. Reinforces the "template + lawyer review" approach the stinger targets.

## Key Facts and Section Checklist

The core sections every SaaS T&C must include (2026 practitioner consensus):

1. **Introduction & Acceptance of Terms** - must clarify users accept by accessing the service
2. **Eligibility & Account Registration** - minimum age, account security responsibility
3. **Subscription, Billing & Payments** - pricing plans, billing cycles, auto-renewal, refund/cancellation policy, taxes
4. **Acceptable Use Policy** - what users cannot do
5. **Intellectual Property Rights** - limited license grant to user, company retains ownership
6. **Data, Privacy & Security Reference** - cross-reference to Privacy Policy; user remains data owner for their content
7. **Service Availability & Modifications** - no uptime guarantee as default; right to modify service
8. **Termination & Suspension** - grounds for termination, data deletion post-termination
9. **Limitation of Liability & Disclaimers** - cap on indirect/consequential damages
10. **Governing Law & Dispute Resolution** - jurisdiction, arbitration vs court

## Key Quotations

- "Your Terms & Conditions (T&C) protect your business, set user expectations, limit liability, and define how your product can be used. Yet most founders either copy them blindly or skip them entirely — both risky moves."
- "⚠️ This generator provides a solid starting point, not legal advice."

## Common Mistakes (for stinger's anti-patterns section)

- Copying T&Cs from competitors blindly
- Ignoring subscription-specific clauses
- Hiding Terms from users (browse-wrap problem)
- Not updating Terms after pricing or feature changes

## Best Practices

- Keep language clear, not overly legal
- Match Terms with actual product behavior
- Review and update as SaaS evolves
- Make Terms easily accessible (signup page, footer)
- Pair with a strong Privacy Policy

## Annotations for stinger-forge

- Directly informs `guides/01-terms-of-service.md` section checklist
- The 10-clause structure is the canonical starting point for the stinger's ToS guide
- Common mistakes section maps to an "anti-patterns" callout box in the guide
